problems: Search in a row-wise and column-wise sorted matrix
schedule.lp_focus: Ownership, Bias for Action
schedule.notes: Focus on fundamental array manipulations and basic search techniques. Understand time/space complexity. Begin thinking about how to take initiative in problem-solving.

schedule.lp_focus: Dive Deep, Insist on the Highest Standards
schedule.notes: Tackle more complex array problems requiring careful logic. Introduce string parsing and manipulation. Pay attention to edge cases and robust solutions.

problems: Length of the longest substring without repeating characters
Detect loop in a linked list
schedule.lp_focus: Learn and Be Curious, Invent and Simplify
schedule.notes: Explore string algorithms like permutations and sliding window. Master linked list basics, including pointer manipulation and cycle detection. Explore different approaches.

problems: Function to check if a singly linked list is palindrome
Check for Balanced Brackets in an expression
schedule.lp_focus: Customer Obsession, Deliver Results
schedule.notes: Advance linked list skills with merge and palindrome checks. Understand stack applications for expression validation and finding patterns. Aim for efficient solutions.

problems: Connect n ropes with minimum cost
schedule.lp_focus: Frugality, Think Big
schedule.notes: Deepen understanding of stack and queue inter-conversions and their applications. Introduce basic greedy approaches for optimization problems.

problems: First negative integer in every window of size k
schedule.lp_focus: Dive Deep, Learn and Be Curious
schedule.notes: Practice queue problems, especially with sliding windows. Start with binary tree traversals (recursive and iterative) and basic properties. Tree-based recursion is crucial.

problems: Lowest Common Ancestor in a Binary Tree
schedule.lp_focus: Invent and Simplify, Are Right, A Lot
schedule.notes: Focus on advanced tree problems, including LCA and diameter. Understand BST properties and how to leverage them for efficient search and retrieval.

problems: Detect cycle in an undirected graph
schedule.lp_focus: Think Big, Deliver Results
schedule.notes: Introduce graph theory with BFS and DFS traversals. Apply these to common problems like finding connected components and cycle detection.

schedule.lp_focus: Dive Deep, Invent and Simplify
schedule.notes: Explore advanced graph algorithms like topological sort and Dijkstra. Understand Trie data structure for efficient string operations.

schedule.lp_focus: Frugality, Bias for Action
schedule.notes: Master heap (priority queue) applications for finding k-th elements and stream processing. Explore hash map usage for frequency counting and window problems.

problems: Search an element in a sorted and rotated array
schedule.lp_focus: Insist on the Highest Standards, Ownership
schedule.notes: Review advanced searching techniques (e.g., binary search variations). Practice efficient sorting algorithms and in-place manipulations.

problems: Rat in a Maze Problem - I
schedule.lp_focus: Learn and Be Curious, Think Big
schedule.notes: Develop strong recursive thinking and backtracking skills. Understand how to explore solution spaces systematically. Practice pruning techniques.

schedule.lp_focus: Dive Deep, Invent and Simplify
schedule.notes: Begin with foundational DP problems. Focus on identifying overlapping subproblems and optimal substructure. Practice memoization and tabulation.

schedule.lp_focus: Are Right, A Lot, Deliver Results
schedule.notes: Continue with intermediate DP problems. Practice recognizing DP patterns in various contexts. Aim for clear state definitions and transitions.

schedule.lp_focus: Frugality, Insist on the Highest Standards
schedule.notes: Tackle more challenging DP problems. Introduce BitMagic for efficient manipulation of numbers. Understand bitwise operators and their applications.

schedule.lp_focus: Dive Deep, Ownership, Earn Trust
schedule.notes: Dedicate time to thoroughly revise your past projects. Be prepared for deep technical grilling on architecture, design choices, trade-offs, and 'what's new'. Prepare STAR stories demonstrating Ownership and Earn Trust.

schedule.lp_focus: Think Big, Customer Obsession, Hire and Develop the Best
schedule.notes: Focus on scalability, reliability, and performance aspects of system design (even if implicit in project discussions). Prepare STAR stories for Customer Obsession and how you've helped others grow.

schedule.lp_focus: Bias for Action, Learn and Be Curious, Deliver Results
schedule.notes: Practice articulating your experiences using the STAR method for LPs like Bias for Action, Learn and Be Curious, and Deliver Results. Review OOPs, DBMS, and OS concepts for MCQ/discussion.

problems: Median of two sorted arrays of different sizes
schedule.lp_focus: Are Right, A Lot, Invent and Simplify
schedule.notes: Solve a mix of medium-to-hard problems across different DSA topics to simulate interview conditions. Practice logical puzzles to sharpen problem-solving skills.

problems: Serialize and Deserialize a Binary Tree
Clone a linked list with next and random pointer
schedule.lp_focus: Insist on the Highest Standards, Dive Deep
schedule.notes: Review challenging problems and concepts. Ensure a strong grasp of your chosen programming language's specifics (e.g., memory management, data structures, concurrency). Identify any weak areas for a quick brush-up.

schedule.lp_focus: All Leadership Principles (holistic assessment)
schedule.notes: Conduct a full mock interview (coding + system design discussion + behavioral). Practice articulating thoughts clearly and concisely. Focus on confidence and demonstrating all LPs. Light review of key concepts.
